text:
To calculate the total cost for Michael to replace the carpet in his bedroom, we need to consider the cost for carpet, padding, removal of the old carpet, and installation of the new carpet.

First, we'll calculate the area of the bedroom:
\[ \text{Area} = \text{Length} \times \text{Width} = 18 \, \text{feet} \times 12 \, \text{feet} = 216 \, \text{square feet} \]

Next, we'll calculate the total cost of the carpet and padding:
\[ \text{Cost for carpet and padding} = (\text{Cost per square foot for carpet} + \text{Cost per square foot for padding}) \times \text{Area} \]
\[ \text{Cost for carpet and padding} = (12 + 2) \times 216 \]
\[ \text{Cost for carpet and padding} = 14 \times 216 \]
\[ \text{Cost for carpet and padding} = 3024 \, \text{dollars} \]

Then, we'll calculate the cost for removing the old carpet:
\[ \text{Cost for removing old carpet} = 4 \times 216 \]
\[ \text{Cost for removing old carpet} = 864 \, \text{dollars} \]

Finally, we'll calculate the cost for installing the new carpet:
\[ \text{Cost for installing new carpet} = 34 \times 216 \]
\[ \text{Cost for installing new carpet} = 7344 \, \text{dollars} \]

Now, we'll add up all the costs to get the total cost:
\[ \text{Total cost} = \text{Cost for carpet and padding} + \text{Cost for removing old carpet} + \text{Cost for installing new carpet} \]
\[ \text{Total cost} = 3024 + 864 + 7344 \]
\[ \text{Total cost} = 11232 \, \text{dollars} \]

So, the total cost for Michael to replace the carpet in his bedroom will be $11,232.
